prompt.scholar: What was the significance of the Battle of Gettysburg (1863)?
prompt.explorer: Why was the Battle of Gettysburg so important?

options.scholar: It was the turning point of the Civil War
options.explorer: It was the turning point of the Civil War

explanation.scholar: Gettysburg (July 1-4, 1863) is widely regarded as the turning point of the Civil War, with 51,116 casualties.
explanation.explorer: Gettysburg was the turning point — after this battle, the Union started winning the war!
